description: Fresh snow and chalk are 'whilo'. A white cloth can be clean or dirty; 'hiso' (clean) makes that separate claim.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue toward the soft palate for voiceless 'wh'. Let air pass without voicing, then move into high-front 'i'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' before rounded mid-back 'o'. Stress falls on 'whi'.
usage_notes: In 'whilo shilu' (pale blue), white lends paleness to the following hue.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: achromatic color at high perceived lightness
semantic_domains.nature: fresh snow and pale clouds give familiar white examples
